other_observers: Seen with Nick Komar Jr and Sr, and Cole Wild; found by Norm Erthal and Joe Roller
specific_location: Along McElmo Creek Road, about 16 miles from US-160
description: the bird was a small oriole, about the same size as nearby Bullock's Orioles. The bill was fairly short, conical with a thin, pointed tip, and a slight decurve. The base of the bill, especially the lower mandible, was blue-gray. The culmen and outer half of the bill was black. The eye was dark. I did not see the leg color.  The cap, nape, and most the head of the bird was a yellowish-orange, which would vary depending on the light from a fairly yellowish color with only a slight orange tinge, to an intense orange with a slight yellowish tinge, approaching the color of nearby Bullock's Orioles, but always with a more purely orange color and no red tinge. The face of the bird, from about the eye and lores to the upper chest were black, forming a black bib. The remainder of the underparts were yellowish-orange, with the same variation as the orange part of the head, though less intense, especially on the lower underparts.  The back was black. The wings were also black, with a singe large white wingbar, and with some white edges to the coverts and flight feathers. The rump and uppertail coverts were orange, and the tail was black.Behaviors: The bird was seen on both occasions high in a cottonwood tree, and did not seem to be associating with other orioles in the area.Call: none heardPlumage: adult alternate
photographer: Photographer: Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, Andrew Spencer, a, Andrew Spencer
